title: How to Properly Set Up a Non-Resident Passthrough LLC in Wyoming
excerpt: Wyoming's LLC structure is among the most favorable in the US for non-residents — no state income tax, strong privacy, and clean passthrough treatment. Here's how to set it up correctly.
html:
Wyoming is the best US state for non-resident LLC formation. No state income tax, no public member registry, and clean federal passthrough treatment make it the default choice for foreign founders who need a US entity without the compliance overhead of Delaware or New York.

$0State income tax
$100Annual report fee
~2 weeksFormation to banking
No SSNRequired to form

Why does Wyoming work for non-residents?

Wyoming has no state income tax and no franchise tax. Unlike Delaware — which charges an annual franchise tax that scales with authorized shares — Wyoming's annual fee is a flat $100 regardless of company size. Wyoming also doesn't publish member names in public records, which matters for founders who want operational privacy.

The state has a strong LLC statute with explicit charging order protections. A creditor who wins a judgment against you personally cannot seize your LLC interest — they can only attach to future distributions. This makes Wyoming structurally superior to most states for asset protection.

How does the passthrough taxation work?

A single-member Wyoming LLC with a non-US owner is treated as a disregarded entity for US federal tax purposes — the LLC itself files no US federal return. Income flows to the owner directly. If you're tax resident in a territorial jurisdiction like the UAE or Panama, foreign-sourced income may not be taxed at all.

Multi-member LLCs default to partnership taxation. Non-US members receiving income effectively connected to a US trade or business must file a 1040-NR. Pure foreign-sourced income passing through a Wyoming LLC is generally not US-taxable for non-resident aliens.

What does the formation process actually look like?

You file Articles of Organization with the Wyoming Secretary of State directly or through a registered agent. You'll need a registered agent with a physical Wyoming address — expect $50–$150/year for this service. The filing fee is $100. No SSN or ITIN is required to form the LLC.

After formation, apply for an EIN from the IRS via Form SS-4 by fax or mail. Non-residents without an SSN use this route — IRS processing takes 4–6 weeks. For banking, Mercury is the most reliable option for non-resident founders and accepts EIN-only applications for Wyoming LLCs.

What are the ongoing compliance requirements?

Wyoming requires one annual report filed with the Secretary of State, due on the first day of the anniversary month of formation. The fee is $100 for LLCs with assets under $250,000 in Wyoming. There is no state income tax return. Federal requirements depend on your residency status and whether the LLC has US-source income.

StateAnnual costMember privacyFranchise taxNon-resident friendly
Wyoming$100PrivateNoneYes
Delaware$300+Semi-private$400+ minimumYes, but costly
New Mexico$0/yearPrivateNoneYes, limited banking
Florida$138.75PublicNoneModerate

Verdict

Wyoming is the right default for non-resident founders who need a clean US entity for SaaS revenue, payment processing, or US client contracts. The privacy protections, zero state income tax, and flat filing fees consistently outperform Delaware for founders who don't need VC investment structures. Form it, get your EIN, open a Mercury account, and move on.

Frequently Asked Questions

Do I need to visit Wyoming to form an LLC there?

No. Formation is entirely online or by mail through the Wyoming Secretary of State. You never need to visit the state.

Can a non-US citizen own a Wyoming LLC?

Yes. There are no citizenship or residency requirements to own a Wyoming LLC. You need a registered agent with a Wyoming address.

Does a Wyoming LLC need to file a US tax return?

A single-member LLC owned by a non-US person with no US-source income typically does not file a US federal return. Multi-member LLCs file Form 1065. Confirm with a US tax attorney for your specific situation.

Can I open a Mercury Bank account with a Wyoming LLC?

Yes. Mercury accepts Wyoming LLCs with a valid EIN and does not require an SSN for non-resident applicants during the standard business account application.
